name: COUNTY HALL EXTENSION 1

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written: 94·37 m AOD (Ground Level box)

units: metres, transcribed verbatim from a typed/hand-lettered 1974 site-investigation log; depth = base of interval below ground

intervals.verbatim: Topsoil and subsoil
Silty clay - light, dark brown & grey - sandy lenses, mottled
annotations: water level 1·500 m marked in Water Levels column
index: LL 51, PL 20, PI 31, CH; M/C 38%

intervals.verbatim: Laminated clay - dark brown & grey - partings of light brown sand
annotations: index: LL 58, PL 22, PI 36, CH; M/C 30%

intervals.verbatim: Clay - reddish brown - fissured, sandy partings
Sand with a trace of gravel and a little silt - brown - lighter in colour at bottom of layer
annotations: water level 7·300 m marked in Water Levels column

intervals.verbatim: Silt & sand grading into sandy silt at bottom of layer - light and dark brown
annotations: water level 9·500 m marked in Water Levels column
notes column: '*No S.A., water was "held" in silt'

intervals.verbatim: Laminated silty clay - reddish brown & grey - lightly varved
Clayey silt - grey - laminated in parts
Laminated silt and clay - grey
annotations: notes column: '**No tests off disturbed samples - difficult to seperate silt and clay'

intervals.verbatim: Silty sand with odd clay partings - brown
Silty clay - dark brown - very sandy - stony
annotations: base of 150mm percussive boring; rotary coring below

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone - yellowish white - micaceous - medium to coarse grained - clay filled fissure from 21.440m to 21.620m
annotations: flushing water lost @ 16·600 m; started coring at 16·560 m

intervals.verbatim: Gritstone - white with yellow staining
Mudstone - yellow, very soft
Gritstone - white with yellow staining - carbonaceous bands from 22.350 to 22.830
annotations: described as gritstone (coarse sandstone); carbonaceous bands span the whole interval 22·350-22·830

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone - yellow, coarse grained
Mudstone - yellow - very soft
Sandstone - yellowish white - coarse grained
Mudstone with sandstone bands - yellow, very soft
Sandstone - yellowish white - coarse grained
Mudstone with sandstone bands - yellow, very soft
Sandstone - yellowish white - micaceous - fine to medium grained
Siltstone with mudstone bands - yellow
Sandstone - yellowish white - micaceous - fine to medium grained - carbonaceous bands from 27.600m to 28.960m
annotations: notes: 'Sample at 28·960 m to 31·500 m scrubbed away'; recovery 0-34%, RQD 0 in lower run

notes: Pages 1-2 are 'Survey Details' location plans (borehole triangulation off the Electricity Sub-Station / coping), page 3 is an OS Sheet NZ 2642 (1972 ed.) location plan - none carry strata. The strata log is BOREHOLE No. 1, pages 4-6. Driller J.B. 150mm percussive boring 22-25.4.74 to 15.750 m (casing 15.490 m), then 50mm rotary water-flush coring 26-30.4.74 to 31.500 m (casing 16.50 m). Depth = running sum of thickness; arithmetic checks out on every interval, no clerk slips found. Bedrock (Pennine/Lower Coal Measures type: sandstone/gritstone/mudstone/siltstone with carbonaceous bands) from 15.750 m; no COAL logged - carbonaceous bands only, so no named seams. Water levels marked at 1·500, 7·300, 9·500 m; flushing water lost @ 16·600 m. Borehole collapsed at 7·130 m after withdrawing casing and was backfilled from 7·130 m with silty sand & clay. Registration marginalia 'NZ 24 SE/98 a-f' spans holes a-f; this record is hole 'a'. BGS blue banner, watermarks and registration stamps ignored per convention.
